mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 1% are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.0% signals persistent labor market slack that weakens wage income and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months when UNRATE > 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a low nominal federal funds rate create negative real interest rates that drive capital out of cash and fixed income into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of joint-signal months, or each proxy alone explains returns without the other (t-test of interaction p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansions, accelerating industrial production reflects normalization of capacity utilization and rising operating leverage, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods classified as early-cycle expansion, INDPRO month-over-month changes fail to achieve a Rank IC above 0.02 for 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirm a broad-based demand expansion that lowers recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of HOUST and PAYEMS fail to outperform a single-proxy model in predicting 60-day returns, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.0%, equity duration risk is elevated; any month-over-month increase in the policy rate triggers a rapid repricing of discount rates and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% and the rate increases month-over-month, or returns are insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales is a coincident indicator of consumer demand strength that positively predicts S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.03 for predicting 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105 for two consecutive months signals manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 105 for two consecutive months, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year backtest.
